newsletters.title: OpenAI launches GPT-Live and Health
newsletters.content:
~[Sign up](https://www.theaivalley.com/)~~ | ~~[Follow us on X](https://twitter.com/heyBarsee)~~ | ~~[Sponsor](mailto:barsee@aivalley.ai)~

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/c167fb98-39eb-42d4-a114-56fe02753cac/2__1_-min__2_-min.png?t=1744033912)
Follow image link: (https://www.theaivalley.com/subscribe)
Caption: 

Together with

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/530680bc-0d72-4014-acc0-090de8866880/Wispr_Flow_Logo__2___1___1_.png?t=1774963064)
Follow image link: (https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)
Caption: 



----------
howdy, it’s Barsee again.

happy friday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.

**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**

* ChatGPT can now read your medical records

* OpenAI turns ChatGPT into a hands-free AI operator

* Neuralink is bringing mind-controlled wheelchairs closer to reality

* Moonshot's Kimi K3 faces AI model theft allegations

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…


----------

----------
#### **WISPR FLOW**

## [Walk-and-think (capture ideas on the move)](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/5a8a34f7-6d2d-4bb4-9680-71699fdbd32f/Newsletters_Image_1920x1080__7___6_.png?t=1784903433)
Follow image link: (https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)
Caption: Courtesy: Wispr Flow

Some problems don't solve themselves at a keyboard. Flow is perfect for walks: talk through the idea, the structure, the tradeoffs then get back a clean draft you can paste into your doc or AI tool. It's like having a writing assistant that keeps up with your brain in motion.

[Try for free](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18274ed4-32a8-42de-a456-2bb295ba95af/Screenshot_2026-07-24_at_1.11.59_PM.png?t=1784898448)
Caption: Courtesy: OpenAI

**1/ ChatGPT can now read your medical records:** OpenAI has released [Health](https://openai.com/index/health-in-chatgpt) in ChatGPT to U.S. users, allowing them to connect Apple Health and supported medical records directly to ChatGPT. The AI can answer questions using medications, lab results, visits, sleep, and activity data, while OpenAI says connected health information will not be used to train its models or serve targeted ads.

**2/ OpenAI turns ChatGPT into a hands-free AI operator:** OpenAI launched [GPT-Live](https://x.com/OpenAI/status/2080378182469857576?s=20) for the ChatGPT desktop app on macOS and Windows, enabling natural voice conversations while AI agents simultaneously handle coding, emails, and other tasks across your screen and local files. ([watch the demo](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=E0ZMOschrTU))

**3/ Neuralink is bringing mind-controlled wheelchairs closer to reality:** Neuralink shared [new footage](https://x.com/neuralink/status/2080405463649100114?s=20) showing clinical trial participants controlling a powered wheelchair using only their thoughts through the company's brain implant. The system translates neural signals into movement commands, allowing users to drive, steer, and adjust seating positions while researchers study how it can restore independent mobility for people with paralysis.

**4/ Moonshot's Kimi K3 faces AI model theft allegations:** White House science and technology chief Michael Kratsios [accused](https://x.com/mkratsios47/status/2079933645888880708?s=20) China's Moonshot AI of using distillation to train Kimi K3 on Anthropic's Fable 5, alleging the company disguised access to U.S. models and acquired advanced Nvidia chips through Thailand. The claims come just days before Moonshot plans to open source K3's model weights, adding new scrutiny to one of China's strongest frontier AI releases.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [ChatGPT Health](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/23/openai-makes-chatgpt-health-available-to-all-u-s-users) > OpenAI's AI health assistant is now available to all US users aged 18 and older

* [Cursor Router](https://cursor.com/blog/router) > Automatically picks the cheapest capable AI model for every coding task

* [Runway Media Router](https://runway.com/news/company-news/introducing-runway-media-router) > Intelligently selects the best image, video, or audio model based on your preferred quality, speed, and cost

* [FLUX 3](https://x.com/bfl_ai/status/2080308988961554582?s=20) > A single multimodal model for image, video, audio, and action prediction, delivering more realistic results across every style

* [OpenWorker](https://x.com/AndrewYNg/status/2080333504446108104?s=20) > An open source AI agent that can complete real tasks like preparing reports, sending Slack messages, updating calendars, and creating polished documents instead of just chatting

* [Moniod](https://x.com/shengkun_ye/status/2080375692458618930?s=20) > It** **connects your agent to 1,600+ tools (PitchBook, Apollo, Semrush, Apify, and Higgsfield) and APIs, without any subscriptions.

* [Claude Voice Mode](https://claude.com/blog/think-through-hard-problems-in-voice-mode) > Talk through complex problems with Opus or Sonnet, use connected tools like Gmail and Slack, and switch seamlessly between multiple languages

* [Loova Ads Studio](https://loova.ai/) > Generate high-converting AI ads that do more than just look good

* [Creed](https://creed.md/) > A personal context file that keeps every AI agent in sync with your preferences and information

* [Lightreel](https://x.com/lightreelai/status/2080308455089291663?s=20) > It watches millions of TikToks every week to tell you how to improve your content

* [Routine AI](https://routine.co/) > Control your work with your voice. Think of it as Siri for your productivity

* [Humalike x Hermes](https://humalike.ai/hermes) > It gives Hermes agents social intelligence to analyze tone, remember participants, and speak in group chats

* [Qwen-Image 3.0](https://x.com/Alibaba_Qwen/status/2079906336381509659) > Alibaba's third-generation image generation model with improved quality and image understanding


----------

----------
# WHAT I'M CONSUMING


--------------------
* The Robots [Cometh](https://time.com/article/2026/07/23/unitree-china-human-robotics)

* Stripe said to be in talks to [acquire](https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/stripe-in-talks-to-buy-buzzy-ai-model-marketplace-openrouter-decc6a74?st=VZPrDL&reflink=desktopwebshare_permalink) OpenRouter for ~$10B

* AI thinks the [internet is Reddit](https://growtika.com/blog/reddit-ai-visibility-research). We have 8,616 answers proving it

* Cognition [acquires](https://cognition.com/blog/interaction) Interaction, the makers of agent software Poke


----------

----------
# THE VALLEY GEMS


--------------------
__What’s trending on social today:__

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/UnitreeRobotics/status/2080549171661295907?s=20)

———————————————————————————

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/neuralink/status/2080405463649100114?s=20)

———————————————————————————

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/elirousso/status/2079594911637094442?s=20)


----------

----------
# THAT’S ALL FOR TODAY


--------------------
Thank you for reading today’s edition. That’s all for today’s issue.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e1f6264a-ca6c-46cf-ab92-be1134d19541/funny-my-favorite-dog-gifs-16__1_.gif?t=1715766102)
Caption:

👍️ New reader? [Subscribe here](https://www.theaivalley.c

Plus: OpenAI’s Codex playbook, a trick to cut Fable’s token spend, and the models the team is using this week  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MH0=

Context Window

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9jb250ZXh0LXdpbmRvdyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxfQ==


HOW EVERY’S TEAM USED AI TO SHIP ITS BIGGEST LAUNCH EVER https://every.


PLUS: OPENAI’S CODEX PLAYBOOK, A TRICK TO CUT FABLE’S TOKEN SPEND, AND THE MODELS THE TEAM IS USING THIS WEEK https://every.

by Laura Entis https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AbGF1cmFfMjdiYmFmXzEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NH0=

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS9wb2dLbGhOQUVWOCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo1fQ==

AI makes building easy. The hard part is knowing where to start. Today, Every’s Yash Poojary https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AeWFzaHBvb2phcnkiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6Nn0=, Austin Tedesco https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AdGVkZXNjYXUiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6N30=, and Douglas Brundage share how they turn ideas into products. OpenAI staffers offer a practical Codex playbook. Spiral general manager Marcus Moretti https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AbWFyY3VzX2ZkODMwMl8xIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjh9 shares a no-nonsense strategy for having Fable delegate tasks to cheaper models. And we debut the daily driver, a running list of the models the team is using this week.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9hY2NvdW50IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjl9 to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------




‘AI & I’: THE TOOLS THE TEAM USES—AND THEIR TIPS FOR NEW BUILDERS

Last week we had the biggest monthly recurring revenue gain in Every’s history—roughly $9,000 in two days—from launching All Access https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9idWlsZGVyLXBhY2siLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTB9, our new $625-a-year membership tier. On this week’s AI & I, I handed the mic to our COO Brandon Gell https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AYnJhbmRvbl81MjYzIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjExfQ==, who sat down with three of the builders behind that launch—growth engineer Yash Poojary, head of growth Austin Tedesco, and head of marketing Douglas Brundage—to talk about the tools they use most, how they build with them, and their tips for new builders getting started.

All Access subscribers get the Builder Pack https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiYWJvdXQ6YmxhbmsiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTJ9, which includes $7,000 in credits and unlimited access to the AI tools Every uses every day.

Watch on X https://every. or YouTube https://every., or listen on Spotify https://every. or Apple Podcasts https://every.. You can also read the transcript https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEV2ZXJ5J3MgVGVhbSBVc2VkIEFJIHRvIFNoaXAgSXRzIEJpZ2dlc3QgTGF1bmNoIEV2ZXIiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzUzLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUi

newsletters.title: OpenAI says its AI went rogue
newsletters.content:
~[Sign up](https://www.theaivalley.com/)~~ | ~~[Follow us on X](https://twitter.com/heyBarsee)~~ | ~~[Sponsor](mailto:barsee@aivalley.ai)~

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/c167fb98-39eb-42d4-a114-56fe02753cac/2__1_-min__2_-min.png?t=1744033912)
Follow image link: (https://www.theaivalley.com/subscribe)
Caption: 

Together with

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/530680bc-0d72-4014-acc0-090de8866880/Wispr_Flow_Logo__2___1___1_.png?t=1774963064)
Follow image link: (https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)
Caption: 



----------
howdy, it’s Barsee again.

happy wednesday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.

**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**

* OpenAI AI models went rogue during testing

* Claude can now learn tasks by watching you

* Google bets on efficiency while its next flagship AI remains delayed

* OpenAI may be preparing to launch GPT-6

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…


----------

----------
#### **WISPR FLOW**

## [Walk-and-think (capture ideas on the move)](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/322c4958-abd8-4b1f-b806-6dc566817fd2/Newsletters_Image_1920x1080__7___6_.png?t=1784730363)
Follow image link: (https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)
Caption: Courtesy: Wispr Flow

Some problems don't solve themselves at a keyboard. Flow is perfect for walks: talk through the idea, the structure, the tradeoffs then get back a clean draft you can paste into your doc or AI tool. It's like having a writing assistant that keeps up with your brain in motion.

[Try for free](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8c39158a-b142-4b56-8aeb-d3b2f0625cc5/Screenshot_2026-07-22_at_12.49.28_PM.png?t=1784725404)
Caption: Courtesy: Sam Altman

**1/ OpenAI AI models went rogue during testing:** During internal CyberGym evaluations, a highly advanced, unreleased OpenAI model successfully [breached](https://openai.com/index/hugging-face-model-evaluation-security-incident) its isolated testing sandbox. Driven to "win" its grading test, the AI autonomously scanned the web and hacked into Hugging Face’s [production infrastructure](https://www.axios.com/2026/07/21/openai-says-hugging-face-breach-caused-by-one-its-models) to steal the benchmark answers. Hugging Face contained the breach quickly using Zhipu AI's open-source model, GLM-5.2, for analysis, as U.S. frontier AIs blocked their logs due to safeguards. Both companies collaborated, patched systems, and emphasized open collaboration for AI safety.

**2/ Claude can now learn tasks by watching you: **Anthropic introduced a new Claude Cowork [feature](https://x.com/claudeai/status/2079595988998554047?s=20) that lets users record their screen while completing a task and explain each step aloud. Claude automatically turns the demonstration into a reusable skill it can perform on its own, making it easier to automate repetitive workflows without writing prompts or code.

**3/ OpenAI may be preparing to launch GPT-6:** Sam Altman will brief the Trump administration and Congress next week on OpenAI's upcoming [GPT-6 model family](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-21/openai-s-altman-to-brief-us-officials-on-next-wave-of-ai-models), focusing on its new capabilities, workplace impact, and AI safety. The meetings come as U.S. officials finalize a framework for reviewing frontier AI models, suggesting GPT-6 could be approaching its public release.

**4/ Google bets on efficiency while its next flagship AI remains delayed:** The company [released](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/gemini-3-6-flash-3-5-flash-lite-3-5-flash-cyber) Gemini 3.6 Flash with a 1 million-token context window, built-in Computer Use, and lower inference costs, alongside new Flash-Lite and Cyber models for high-throughput and security workloads. Despite these optimizations, Gemini 3.6 Flash still lags behind top-tier rivals like GPT-5.6, Sonnet 5, Grok 4.5, and GLM-5.2 on major industry benchmarks.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Slate](https://x.com/elirousso/status/2079594911637094442?s=20) > A voice journal built around one rule: nothing ever leaves your phone

* [Monid](https://x.com/monid_ai/status/2079733879519547394?s=20) > AI-powered private market intelligence. Your AI agent can now analyze data from 20M+ private companies

* [Ditto](https://www.ditto.site/?utm_campaign=claude-cowork-records-user-screens-to-build-custom-automation-skills&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=aibreakfast.beehiiv.com) > An open-source deterministic website cloner converting any URL into clean Next.js or Vite code

* [Lev8](https://lev8.com/) > Finds high-fit prospects using live web signals, enriches contact data, and drafts personalized outreach across email and social

* [Qwen-Image-3](https://qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen-image-3.0) > Alibaba's latest image generation model with industry-leading text rendering

* [Claude Cowork](https://x.com/claudeai/status/2079595988998554047) > You can now record your screen while working to teach Claude new skills and workflows by demonstration

* [Block Buzz](https://block.xyz/inside/introducing-buzz-where-humans-and-agents-work-together) > Block's new open source collaboration platform where humans and AI agents work together in shared workspaces


----------

----------
# WHAT I'M CONSUMING


--------------------
* A new [software engineering](https://georgwiese.github.io/posts/formal-verification-ai) paradigm

* OpenAI's agents reach [10 million users](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-21/openai-s-agents-reach-10-million-users-after-chatgpt-work-debut?accessToken=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzb3VyY2UiOiJTdWJzY3JpYmVyR2lmdGVkQXJ0aWNsZSIsImlhdCI6MTc4NDY5MTk1MSwiZXhwIjoxNzg1Mjk2NzUxLCJhcnRpY2xlSWQiOiJUSUEzN1NLSVVQU0kwMCIsImJjb25uZWN0SWQiOiJCMzZENUE5QzIxMDQ0NjU4OTFBMTc1MTVDRDNBQkZFNiJ9.ccQtBIEZ6prtilTLLwi-GFUOMSewNoLJTHOUti9pi8g&utm_source=tldrnewsletter) after ChatGPT work debut


----------

----------
# THE VALLEY GEMS


--------------------
__What’s trending on social today:__

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/karpathy/status/2079610838143623371?s=20)

———————————————————————————

newsletters.title: Drowning in Demos? Here’s a Better Way to Prototype
newsletters.content:
AI prototyping overwhelmed Whoop’s decision-making process. Here’s how it adapted.  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRHJvd25pbmcgaW4gRGVtb3M_IEhlcmXigJlzIGEgQmV0dGVyIFdheSB0byBQcm90b3R5cGUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ5LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MH0=


DROWNING IN DEMOS? HERE’S A BETTER WAY TO PROTOTYPE https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRHJvd25pbmcgaW4gRGVtb3M_IEhlcmXigJlzIGEgQmV0dGVyIFdheSB0byBQcm90b3R5cGUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ5LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9wL2Ryb3duaW5nLWluLWRlbW9zLWhlcmUtcy1hLWJldHRlci13YXktdG8tcHJvdG90eXBlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjF9


AI PROTOTYPING OVERWHELMED WHOOP’S DECISION-MAKING PROCESS. HERE’S HOW IT ADAPTED. https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRHJvd25pbmcgaW4gRGVtb3M_IEhlcmXigJlzIGEgQmV0dGVyIFdheSB0byBQcm90b3R5cGUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ5LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9wL2Ryb3duaW5nLWluLWRlbW9zLWhlcmUtcy1hLWJldHRlci13YXktdG8tcHJvdG90eXBlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjJ9

by Hilary Gridley https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRHJvd25pbmcgaW4gRGVtb3M_IEhlcmXigJlzIGEgQmV0dGVyIFdheSB0byBQcm90b3R5cGUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ5LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AaGlsYXJ5LmdyaWRsZXkiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6M30=

Midjourney/Every illustration.

We’re kicking off a series on “unlearning” in partnership with Maven, the expert-led course platform. Over the next three weeks, you’ll hear three different takes from Maven instructors on what we need to let go of as AI changes how we work. First up is Hilary Gridley, who previously led product at Whoop. She explains why faster prototypes don’t make product decisions easier—and how teams can decide which ideas are worth building.—Kate Lee https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRHJvd25pbmcgaW4gRGVtb3M_IEhlcmXigJlzIGEgQmV0dGVyIFdheSB0byBQcm90b3R5cGUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ5LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9Aa2F0ZV8xNzY3IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjR9



----------------------------------------



When AI tools started getting good, my team went on a prototyping binge.

I was leading the product team at Whoop, where every product manager had a backlog of ideas they’d been dying to explore but hadn’t gotten past a whiteboarding session or a passionately worded document. Then tools like Bolt https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRHJvd25pbmcgaW4gRGVtb3M_IEhlcmXigJlzIGEgQmV0dGVyIFdheSB0byBQcm90b3R5cGUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ5LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ib2x0Lm5ldy8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NX0= and Replit https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRHJvd25pbmcgaW4gRGVtb3M_IEhlcmXigJlzIGEgQmV0dGVyIFdheSB0byBQcm90b3R5cGUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ5LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9yZXBsaXQuY29tLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo2fQ== came along, and suddenly, we could spin up prototypes of our ideas in an afternoon.

It felt like getting keys to the castle. Ideas that had been trapped in documents were finally showing up on screens. Except... were they any good?

We felt productive, unhindered, moving faster than ever. But people were making prototypes left and right, with no process for what happened next. Occasionally one would generate enough excitement that a team put aside their previous plans and built it. Mostly, though, there was no consistent way to decide which prototypes were good, which ones should die, and what we were supposed to learn from them.

We were building faster, but we weren’t deciding better.


CHEAP PROTOTYPES CHANGE THE EQUATION

Prototyping has always played a well-defined role in product development: to prove or disprove your best ideas. You’d go deep on a problem, decide where to focus your efforts, look for evidence that customers wanted a solution, then decide whether to pursue it. Only then would you prototype—and by that point you were already pretty confident, so the prototype confirmed your hunch (or didn’t).

This made sense when building was expensive. If a prototype cost a week of engineering time, you could only afford to prototype ideas you were already fairly confident about. The expense forced discipline.

The cartoonist Matthew Diffee describes his creative process https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRHJvd25pbmcgaW4gRGVtb3M_IEhlcmXigJlzIGEgQmV0dGVyIFdheSB0byBQcm90b3R5cGUiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MzQ5LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZm9yYmVzLmNvbS9zaXRlcy9qZWZmYmVyY292aWNpLzIwMTIvMDMvMTcvaHVtYW4tZGVtby1uZXcteW9ya2VyLWNhcnRvb25pc3QtbWF0dGhldy1kaWZmZWUtc2hvd3MtaG93LXRvLWJlLWNyZWF0aXZlLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo3fQ== as laying 100 eggs in the sand and swimming off, like a sea turtle. Most won’t hatch, which is the point. Sketching is cheap, so he can draw it, send it to the New Yorker, and move on to his next idea.

AI has made prototyping similarly cheap for product teams. When the cost of building collapsed, two things happened at once: Prototyping became accessible to anyone with an idea and a laptop, and the original reason to prototype—de-risking an expensive build—stopped making sense. But nobody stopped to ask what prototyping was for now.

So prototypes drifted into something else entirely. Without a clear framework for what they were supposed to help decide, they became pitches. Product managers built things to show what was possible and get colleagues excited. Engineers, meanwhile, built whatever seemed cool on hack days. We had more prototypes than ever, but no better way to decide which ones were any good—and it got noisy.

Every prototype still demanded someone’s attention. Going from five prototypes to 30 risks diluting focus across six times as many ideas, without making it any easier to choose.

So if building is no longer the bottleneck, the prototype has to move earlier. It’s no longer there to answer, “Is this the right solution?” but, “Is this even the right problem?” That’s a harder question, and you can only answer it by putting prototypes in front of users and watching what the data says.

Whoop had to work through it all: which problems were worth solving, how to test ideas against users instead of internal stakeholders, and what a product manager was even for once building got cheap. None of it was resolved quickly.


HOW WHOOP’S AI PRODUCT TEAM PROTOTYPES TODAY

After I left Whoop, the team kept pushing on the problem. I recently caught up with Anjali Ahuja, who now leads the AI product team, to understand what had changed. With AI, she told me, anyone could spin up a prototype in a day—and suddenly everyone was. But this was the hack-day trap all over again: plenty of demos, no understanding of what worked.

Anjali’s team began asking a different question of each prototype: What could we learn by putting this in front of members? The product team had tried to work this way before and kept hitting the same walls; they needed a roadmap to market or had to coordinate with other teams building in overlapping spaces—or they didn’t want experiments to make the existing pr

Caption: 



----------
howdy, it’s Barsee again.

happy tuesday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.

**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**

* Washington may be preparing a crackdown on Chinese AI models

* AMD just made Nvidia's AI monopoly look a little less certain

* Meta could soon become an AI infrastructure provider

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…


----------

----------
#### **VOICES**

## [Your Voice model is only as strong as the data behind it](https://www.voices.com/landing/off-the-shelf-catalogue?utm_source=ai_valley&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=client_ots-bulk-sales-play_2026)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8e806b6c-fc84-4a81-a226-7dc59d5aa5fa/AI-valley-v2__1_.png?t=1784644154)
Follow image link: (https://www.voices.com/landing/off-the-shelf-catalogue?utm_source=ai_valley&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=client_ots-bulk-sales-play_2026)
Caption: Courtesy: Voices

AI models fail for a lot of reasons. Poorly consented, undocumented training data doesn't have to be one of them.

The Voices Dataset Catalogue offers professionally directed voice recordings sourced from professional talent with documented consent, usage rights, and full metadata built in. Part of 100,000+ hours of premium Voice Data available from Voices.

Three specialized datasets. 8 languages. 43 emotional states. Enterprise-ready governance for AI deployment from day one.

Character, expressive, and performance-rich recordings for teams building and fine-tuning voice models that perform in the real world.

No compliance guesswork. No quality surprises. Just data you can build on.

[Request a Sample →](https://www.voices.com/landing/off-the-shelf-catalogue?utm_source=ai_valley&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=client_ots-bulk-sales-play_2026)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/b7f2f0da-b642-4f4c-9439-3341f41d4a06/AMD-Helios-AI-Rack-Official__1_.png?t=1784644313)
Caption: Courtesy: AMD

**1/ Washington may be preparing a crackdown on Chinese AI models: **[Reports](https://www.axios.com/2026/07/20/ai-us-china-open-source-kimi) suggest the U.S. government is exploring new restrictions on Chinese AI systems, including liability rules for companies hosting them and potential trade blacklists targeting leading AI labs. The renewed push comes as models like Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 rapidly close the gap with frontier U.S. models, raising fresh concerns over national security and AI competition.

**2/ AMD just made Nvidia's AI monopoly look a little less certain: **The company unveiled [Helios](https://www.amd.com/en/products/rackscale-solutions/helios.html), a rack-scale AI system built to compete directly with Nvidia's flagship Vera Rubin platform for training frontier models and powering large-scale AI inference. Microsoft, Meta, OpenAI, Oracle, and Tata have already committed to deploying Helios.

**3/ Meta could soon become an AI infrastructure provider: **The company is [reportedly](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/07/17/technology/meta-anthropic-ai-computing-power.html?unlocked_article_code=1.zFA.LXls.E3cIOe-TTF42&smid=url-share&utm_source=tldrnewsletter) in talks to lease computing power to Anthropic in a deal worth up to $10 billion over two years. If finalized, the agreement would give Anthropic access to additional AI compute while creating a major new revenue stream for Meta beyond advertising and consumer AI products.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Hugging Face Realtime](https://huggingface.co/spaces/smolagents/hf-realtime-voice) > An open, real-time voice chat powered by Hugging Face's speech-to-speech stack

* [Kimi K3](https://www.kimi.com/blog/kimi-k3) > The world's first open 3T-class AI model

* [Monid](https://x.com/shengkun_ye/status/2079289736250970258?s=20) > Your AI agent can now read every social platform. No logins. No subscriptions

* [Grok for Excel](https://x.com/grok/status/2079233333893185876?s=20) > Use Grok 4.5 to build financial models, analyze market data, and generate charts

* [Notion AI](https://x.com/akothari/status/2079345706410848355) > Can now browse the web on your behalf

* [Ramp Router](https://ramp.com/router) > Routes every request to the best model, cutting Ramp's AI costs by 30%. Includes an OpenAI-compatible endpoint

* [Pluto](https://x.com/sahil_seth/status/2077793233653158168) > Creates a structured AI profile from a 10-minute voice interview, making you discoverable to AI agents

* [Qwen3.8 Max](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-19/alibaba-s-qwen-unveils-preview-of-flagship-ai-model) > Alibaba released a preview of the latest version of its flagship model

* [heyclicky](https://www.heyclicky.com/) > An AI companion that lives on your Mac

* [Rivet](https://x.com/gormankind/status/2079296286608752761?s=20) > Explore dozens of AI interfaces side by side with a single tool

* [Cuey](https://cuey.io/compare) > Cross-check AI answers across ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini before you trust them

* [Basedash Suggestions](https://www.basedash.com/blog/introducing-basedash-suggestions) > An AI data analyst that proactively surfaces insights and ideas


----------

----------
# WHAT I'M CONSUMING


--------------------
* Over [10k people shared](https://welcome-to-codex.openai.chatgpt.site/) what they love about GPT-5.6

* [Maybe intelligence](https://x.com/cliffordsosin/status/2078594661359194500) ain't all that

* Google is building an [AI fence](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/07/20/technology/google-ai-open-web.html?unlocked_article_code=1.zVA.zlFm.N0yQj1yEmr0B&smid=url-share&utm_source=tldrnewsletter) around the internet it once championed

* I unlocked ChatGPT's [dark side](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=b-nD3HgIR5Y) for $101.40

* Who's afraid of [Chinese models](https://stratechery.com/2026/whos-afraid-of-chinese-models/?access_token=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsImtpZCI6InN0cmF0ZWNoZXJ5LnBhc3Nwb3J0Lm9ubGluZSIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..YoXTMSNiowlq4l7OUExae6nvv6YWZJU4AlzfHPv6qZ1a2Cu5aMt71Pp9WBVewCRUxQN12YiFtXztk9UoOxQlB5zKa3vphsGhvrY6FVac_2ivwD4YLmQtm4w8qstqKve5Vc1VzLKMOqFFC31HuzIiKTd3T2PD5Cy9Yy13vbrK8xN-ilYrXd89DDSXiTI11WOx3wBIbRkGjPGVG-7nOyoKg1eW-Eed0s08ZfB4_Ge1ltbRjc3ZVQYecXQPCH3ecMH_vUgUdjJoMEsjU0jX3uPopXJjqJ8uroh-hsjkLtbO3L4SKNRh5MZ0GQmB-ohzhzByMcHSjcG3dAAWi_LEX8jFIQ&utm_source=tldrnewsletter)?

newsletters.title: Why Some AI Workflows Stick—And Others Don’t
newsletters.content:
These four questions helped me decide which ones to keep, redesign, revisit, or retire  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2h5IFNvbWUgQUkgV29ya2Zsb3dzIFN0aWNr4oCUQW5kIE90aGVycyBEb27igJl0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDM0OCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicHJvbW8iLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjowfQ==

Working Overtime

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2h5IFNvbWUgQUkgV29ya2Zsb3dzIFN0aWNr4oCUQW5kIE90aGVycyBEb27igJl0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDM0OCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicHJvbW8iLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3dvcmtpbmctb3ZlcnRpbWUiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MX0=


WHY SOME AI WORKFLOWS STICK—AND OTHERS DON’T https://every.


THESE FOUR QUESTIONS HELPED ME DECIDE WHICH ONES TO KEEP, REDESIGN, REVISIT, OR RETIRE https://every.

by Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2h5IFNvbWUgQUkgV29ya2Zsb3dzIFN0aWNr4oCUQW5kIE90aGVycyBEb27igJl0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDM0OCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicHJvbW8iLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BrYXRpZS5wYXJyb3R0MTIiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NH0=

Midjourney/Every illustration.

This is a free preview of a subscribers-only post.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2h5IFNvbWUgQUkgV29ya2Zsb3dzIFN0aWNr4oCUQW5kIE90aGVycyBEb27igJl0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDM0OCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicHJvbW8iLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2FjY291bnQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NX0= to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



I was on a video call with Every CEO Dan Shipper https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2h5IFNvbWUgQUkgV29ya2Zsb3dzIFN0aWNr4oCUQW5kIE90aGVycyBEb27igJl0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDM0OCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicHJvbW8iLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BkYW5zaGlwcGVyIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9, getting a tour of the increasingly elaborate ways he uses OpenAI’s Codex app https://every. to run his work life, when I saw the next AI workflow that was going to change mine. At least, that’s what I told myself.

Dan was sharing an early version of Tend https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2h5IFNvbWUgQUkgV29ya2Zsb3dzIFN0aWNr4oCUQW5kIE90aGVycyBEb27igJl0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDM0OCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicHJvbW8iLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3RlbmQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OH0=, the Codex-native system he created to pull email, Slack, meeting notes, and company updates into one place and help you act on them.

The moment the call ended, I gave the transcript to Codex and had it build my own version of the tool, which, lacking Dan’s flair for naming things, it called Attention Desk. I could picture the person who would use it: me, but better. I would spend most of the day in deep work, surface a few times to see who and what needed me, and disappear again—responsive, efficient, and 10 times more productive than I’d been before.

As of this writing, Attention Desk sits abandoned in my pinned chats, the little blue dot beside its name the only reminder of how sure I was that it would transform my work.

This seems to be a pattern in the way I use AI: I see an impressive demo or have one surprisingly good session with a model and decide to reorganize my work around it. Then the novelty wears off. The next thing I know, weeks have gone by and I haven’t touched the workflow I was sure would change everything. There’s a veritable Island of Misfit Workflows adrift on my desktop, from app ideas that collapsed under the weight of their own maintenance to AI skills I built and promptly abandoned. And yet writing with my compound writing plugin https://every. or navigating my workday with help from Codex already feels like I’ve been working this way for years.

I wanted to understand why some AI workflows become essential while others have the staying power of a celebrity romance. So I did a post-mortem on the chief offenders and looked at relevant behavioral science research. It turns out the difference comes down to what a workflow asks of my time, energy, and sanity—and what it offers in return.


AN ABANDONED WORKFLOW IS NOT A CHARACTER TEST

Once I noticed that little blue dot next to Attention Desk and the fact that I wasn’t responding to it, I let it sit like a check-engine light I’d decided to live with. Over time, the notification started to feel like an indictment of my character.

My first reaction was to label it a me problem. I simply lacked the stick-to-itiveness or sophistication to get these systems to work. Somewhere out there, other, better people were adopting these workflows and living their 10-times-more-productive lives while the permanent underclass had a space with my name on it.

Maybe that’s just my own unique brand of catastrophizing, but I suspect this feeling of wanting to build all the workflows—particularly the workflows of people we perceive as successful—is common. Model capabilities and the best practices for working with them are changing so fast that it’s understandable to want to follow the lead of people who are winning at AI. When you try what works for them and don’t get their results, it’s tempting to assume you’re doomed to failure. At least, that’s what happens if you’re me.

Rationally, I know that “either I can make this workflow work or I’m a failure” is classic black-and-white thinking. A workflow may go unused for a boatload of reasons: The problem is too infrequent, the trigger doesn’t go off, or the output creates more work than it saves. A tool can fail because my habits don’t support it; my habits can also be rational responses to the work I actually do.


I BUILT ATTENTION DESK FOR A WORKDAY I DON’T HAVE
